result:
Build is clean (only pre-existing warnings). Per instructions, I won't re-run the test.

## Report

**Root cause:** `classend` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:573-592` parsed the character class `[^]]` (negated class containing literal `]`) incorrectly. The C reference uses a `do-while` that unconditionally consumes the first character after `[` or `[^` before checking for `]` as terminator. The Rust port instead checked-then-consumed, so it treated the very first `]` after `[^` as the class terminator — returning end-position 3 instead of 4 for `[^]]`. This caused `matchbracketclass` to receive `ec=2` (empty range), so the negated class matched every byte — including `]` — and `f(']]]áb', '[^]]+')` returned `']]]áb'` instead of `'áb'`.

**Fix:** Restructured the loop body to read the current char (treating `]` as literal class content on the first iteration), then check the *next* position for `]` as the terminator — matching the C `do-while` semantics. Verified traces against `[^]]`, `[]]`, `[a-z]`, `[%a]`, `[a` (malformed), and `[%` (malformed).

**New state:** Did not re-run the test per instructions; `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean. The mega-loop will re-test.
